title: Collaboration network structure and institutional centrality in Uganda's clinical trial ecosystem
body: How concentrated is the research collaboration network in Uganda, and which institutions bridge disconnected groups? We built an institution-institution co-occurrence network from 783 interventional trials on ClinicalTrials.gov with Ugandan sites, linking sponsors sharing disease categories. Degree centrality, betweenness centrality, clustering coefficient, and a degree Gini index were computed to characterize hub-and-spoke structure. The network contained 222 institutions and over thirteen thousand edges with density 0.56 and average clustering 0.92, indicating a highly interconnected core. Makerere University had the highest degree centrality at 0.96, connecting to 212 of 222 institutions, while US academic centres occupied the next-highest positions. The degree Gini of 0.25 suggested moderately distributed connectivity rather than a single-star topology. Temporal analysis showed increasing density and diversity from 2000 onward. The co-occurrence approach cannot distinguish deep collaboration from coincidental overlap, and registry data may undercount African-led trials.

sentences.text: How concentrated is the research collaboration network in Uganda's clinical trial ecosystem, and which institutions serve as structural bridges?
We analysed 783 interventional trials registered on ClinicalTrials.gov with Ugandan sites, constructing an institution-institution network via condition co-occurrence bipartite projection.
Graph metrics including degree centrality, betweenness centrality, clustering coefficient, and a degree Gini index were computed in pure Python to characterise network topology.
The 222-node network had density 0.56 and average clustering 0.92, with Makerere University holding degree centrality of 0.96, connecting to 212 of 222 institutions.
The degree Gini of 0.25 indicated moderately distributed connectivity, and temporal analysis showed increasing density after 2000.
Uganda's trial network is highly interconnected with Makerere as a non-bottleneck hub, but US academic institutions remain the dominant international partners.
Condition co-occurrence cannot distinguish deep from coincidental collaboration, and ClinicalTrials.gov may undercount African-led studies.

outsideNote.data: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, 783 Uganda trials, 222 institutions

ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used in early drafting. The final text was rewritten by the author, who takes full responsibility for the content.
